channel.title: Xinyun Zettelkasten — Permanent Notes
channel.description: Refined ideas that survived the filter.

item.title: The Question Behind the Question
item.description: Engineers solve problems. The harder skill is knowing which problem to solve — and recognizing that some decisions make entire categories of problems disappear.

item.title: The Door Was Already Open
item.description: What a week of debugging taught me about resistance, reframing, and finding the entrance that was there all along

item.title: The Workaround and the Source
item.description: When working harder is a workaround for working smarter — on comfort zones, visibility, and the architecture of how you show up.

item.title: The Systems That Watch Back
item.description: What happens when the thinking frameworks you build start revealing things about you that you didn't intend to see.

item.title: You Can't Build Trust Directly
item.description: Trust, clarity, and wisdom don't get built by aiming at them. They're the residue of something else — something engineers are uniquely positioned to understand.

item.title: When Knowing Isn't Enough
item.description: Four moments from one week that revealed the gap between knowing a principle and living it — in engineering, in thinking, and in the decisions that actually matter.

item.title: 我如何用一台笔记本和一部手机，搭建了一个会自己进化的工作系统
item.description: A software engineer's personal system architecture — five layers from knowledge to mobile access, with self-improving automation in between. Written in Chinese.

item.title: The Config File That Knows Me Better Than My Mom
item.description: What happens when an engineer starts documenting herself instead of just her systems. On self-knowledge, automation, and why a terminal message at 1am felt warmer than a phone call.

item.title: The System That Builds Itself
item.description: I tried to design a writing habit. The habit designed the writing instead.

item.title: Zettelkasten Method
item.description: A personal knowledge management system using fleeting notes, literature notes, and permanent notes to build a network of interconnected ideas.
